context:
People in this club who perform in school talent shows often attend and are very engaged with school events.
People in this club either perform in school talent shows often or are inactive and disinterested community members.
People in this club who chaperone high school dances are not students who attend the school.
All people in this club who are inactive and disinterested members of their community chaperone high school dances.
All young children and teenagers in this club who wish to further their academic careers and educational opportunities are students who attend the school. 
Bonnie is in this club and she either both attends and is very engaged with school events and is a student who attends the school or is not someone who both attends and is very engaged with school events and is not a student who attends the school.
conclusion: Bonnie performs in school talent shows often.
label: Uncertain
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
clubMember(x) ::: x is a member of the club
performsTalentShow(x) ::: x performs in school talent shows often
engagedWithSchoolEvents(x) ::: x attends and is very engaged with school events
student(x) ::: x is a student who attends the school
inactiveDisinterestedCommunityMember(x) ::: x is an inactive and disinterested community member
chaperoneHighSchoolDance(x) ::: x chaperones high school dances

PREMISES:
forall x (clubMember(x) -> (performsTalentShow(x) v engagedWithSchoolEvents(x)))
forall x (inactiveDisinterestedCommunityMember(x) -> chaperoneHighSchoolDance(x))
forall x (youngChildOrTeenager(x) -> student(x))
clubMember(bonnie)

CONCLUSION:
performsTalentShow(bonnie)

context:
People in this club who perform in school talent shows often attend and are very engaged with school events.
People in this club either perform in school talent shows often or are inactive and disinterested community members.
People in this club who chaperone high school dances are not students who attend the school.
All people in this club who are inactive and disinterested members of their community chaperone high school dances.
All young children and teenagers in this club who wish to further their academic careers and educational opportunities are students who attend the school. 
Bonnie is in this club and she either both attends and is very engaged with school events and is a student who attends the school or is not someone who both attends and is very engaged with school events and is not a student who attends the school.
conclusion: If Bonnie is either both a young child or teenager who wishes to further her academic career and educational opportunities and chaperones high school dances or neither is a young child nor teenager who wishes to further her academic career and educational opportunities, then Bonnie is either a student who attends the school or is an inactive and disinterested member of the community.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
club_member(x) ::: x is a member of the club
perform_in_talent_show(x) ::: x performs in school talent shows
attend_school_events(x) ::: x attends and is engaged with school events
chaperone_high_school_dance(x) ::: x chaperones high school dances
young_child_teenager(x) ::: x is a young child or teenager
wish_further_academic_career(x) ::: x wishes to further academic career and educational opportunities
student_attends_school(x) ::: x is a student who attends the school
inactive_disinterested_community_member(x) ::: x is an inactive and disinterested community member

PREMISES:
forall x (club_member(x) -> (perform_in_talent_show(x) -> attend_school_events(x)))
forall x (club_member(x) -> (chaperone_high_school_dance(x) -> NOT student_attends_school(x)))
forall x (young_child_teenager(x) AND wish_further_academic_career(x) -> student_attends_school(x))
club_member(bonnie)
(bonnie -> (young_child_teenager(bonnie) AND wish_further_academic_career(bonnie) -> student_attends_school(bonnie)))
(bonnie -> (NOT young_child_teenager(bonnie) AND wish_further_academic_career(bonnie) -> inactive_disinterested_community_member(bonnie)))

CONCLUSION:
(bonnie -> (young_child_teenager(bonnie) OR NOT young_child_teenager(bonnie)) -> (student_attends_school(bonnie) OR inactive_disinterested_community_member(bonnie)))

context:
All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today. 
Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers. 
Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home.
All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home. 
No managers work remotely from home. 
James will appear in the company today if and only if he is a manager.
conclusion: James has lunch in the company.
label: Uncertain
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
employee(x) ::: x is an employee
schedule_meeting(x) ::: x schedules a meeting with their customers
go_to_company_building(x) ::: x goes to the company building
has_lunch_in_company_building(x) ::: x has lunch in the company building
working_remotely_from_home(x) ::: x works remotely from home
in_other_countries(x) ::: x is in other countries
manager(x) ::: x is a manager

PREMISES:
forall x (employee(x) -> schedule_meeting(x) -> go_to_company_building(x))
forall x (has_lunch_in_company_building(x) -> schedule_meeting(x))
forall x (employee(x) -> (has_lunch_in_company_building(x) XOR working_remotely_from_home(x)))
forall x (in_other_countries(x) -> working_remotely_from_home(x))
not manager(James)
James = manager

CONCLUSION:
has_lunch_in_company_building(James)

context:
All employees who schedule a meeting with their customers will go to the company building today. 
Everyone who has lunch in the company building schedules meetings with their customers. 
Employees have lunch either in the company building or at home.
If an employee has lunch at home, they are working remotely from home.
All employees who are in other countries work remotely from home. 
No managers work remotely from home. 
James will appear in the company today if and only if he is a manager.
conclusion: If James is either a manager or in other countries,  then James does not either has lunch at home or work remotely from home. If James either has lunch at home or works remotely from home, then he is neither a manager nor does he work in other countries. [contrapositive is more natural]
label: True

in_other_countries(x) ::: x is in other countries
manager(x) ::: x is a manager

forall x (lunch_in_company_building(x) -> schedule_meeting(x))
forall x (employee(x) -> (lunch_in_company_building(x) v work_remotely_from_home(x)))
forall x (in_other_countries(x) -> work_remotely_from_home(x))
forall x (manager(x) -> NOT work_remotely_from_home(x))
forall x (employee(x) -> (manager(x) v in_other_countries(x)) -> NOT (lunch_in_company_building(x) v work_remotely_from_home(x)))

CONCLUSION:
(manager(James) v in_other_countries(James)) -> NOT (lunch_in_company_building(James) v work_remotely_from_home(James))
(lunch_in_company_building(James) v work_remotely_from_home(James)) -> NOT (manager(James) v in_other_countries(James))

CONTRAPOSITIVE:
NOT (manager(James) v in_other_countries(James)) -> (lunch_in_company_building(James) v work_remotely_from_home(James))
NOT (lunch_in_company_building(James) v work_remotely_from_home(James)) -> (manager(James) v in_other_countries(James))

context:
In superhero movies, the good guys always win.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is a superhero movie.
Good guys fight bad guys and vice versa.
Sir Digby fights his nemesis.
If a superhero movie is named after a character, that character is a good guy.
The Surprising Adventures of Sir Digby Chicken Caesar is named after Sir Digby.
If somebody wins a fight, the person they are fighting does not win.
If a superhero movie is named after a character, that character is in the movie.
conclusion: Sir Digby’s nemesis does not win.
label: True
formalisations:

good_guy(x) ::: x is a good guy
bad_guy(x) ::: x is a bad guy
fights(x, y) ::: x fights y
win_fight(x, y) ::: x wins the fight against y
named_after(x, y) ::: x is named after y
in_movie(x, y) ::: x is in the movie y

fights(sir_digby, nemesis)
win_fight(sir_digby, nemesis)

in_movie(surprising_adventures_of_sir_digby_chicken_caesar, sir_digby)

CONCLUSION:
not win_fight(nemesis, sir_digby)

A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
The security deposit can be either equal to the monthly rent at a managed building or more.

Cats are pets.
The Olive Garden is a managed building.
The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
$2000 is more than $1500.
Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy, and the security deposit is no more than $1500.

conclusion: Tom will rent an apartment in The Olive Garden.

more_than(x, y) ::: x is more than y
fluffy(x) ::: x is Fluffy
tom(x) ::: x is Tom
olive_garden(x) ::: x is The Olive Garden

cat(fluffy)
pet(cat)

rent_apartment(tom, olive_garden)

context:
If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try are people who spill a lot of food on their clothing.
If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
Peter is either a person who spills a lot of food on his clothing and is cleanly, or a person who does not spill a lot of food on his clothing and is not cleanly
conclusion: Peter is either both a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try and issomeone whose family prioritizes order and spotlessness, or he neither is a clumsy foodie who goes out frequently to find new food restaurants to try nor does his family prioritize order and spotlessness.
label: True

cleanly(x) ::: x is cleanly
value_order_spotlessness(x) ::: x values order and spotlessness
family_prioritize_order_spotlessness(x) ::: x's family prioritizes order and spotlessness

PREMISES:

forall x (cleanly(x) -> tidy(x))
forall x (value_order_spotlessness(x) -> family_prioritize_order_spotlessness(x))

CONCLUSION:
(clumsy_foodie(Peter) AND family_prioritize_order_spotlessness(Peter)) OR (NOT clumsy_foodie(Peter) AND NOT family_prioritize_order_spotlessness(Peter))

context:
All people who make wedding plans are people who are engaged.
All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.
Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.
Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
If John is engaged, then John does not invite friends to their ceremony and make wedding plans.
If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
conclusion: John has a larger family.
label: False
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
engaged(x) ::: x is engaged
wedding_plans(x) ::: x makes wedding plans
invites_others(x) ::: x invites others to their ceremony
well_attended_wedding(x) ::: x has a well-attended wedding
larger_family(x) ::: x has a larger family
invites_friends(x) ::: x invites friends to their ceremony

PREMISES:
forall x (engaged(x) -> wedding_plans(x))
forall x (invites_others(x) -> wedding_plans(x))
well_attended_wedding(john) -> invites_others(john)
larger_family(john) -> (well_attended_wedding(john) v invites_friends(john))
NOT invites_friends(john)

CONCLUSION:
larger_family(john)

If a film contains excessive erotic and violent content, children cannot watch it without guidance from their parents.

All family-friendly animated films are rated General Audience.
All movies in the Frozen series are family-friendly animated films.
“Hachi: A dog’s Tale” is a film.
“Hachi: A dog’s Tale”  is either a family-friendly animated film or is appropriate for people of all ages.

film(x) ::: x is a film

If someone has United States citizenship, then they can register to vote in the United States.
A person either has United States citizenship or Taiwanese citizenship.
No Russian Federation officials hold Taiwanese citizenship. 
Vladimir neither holds Taiwanese citizenship nor is he a manager at Gazprom. 
Ekaterina she can register to vote in the United States, or she is a Russian federation official.
conclusion: Ekaterina can participate in the 2024 United States presidential election or is a manager at Gazprom.
label: True
formalisations:
PREDICATES:
register_to_vote(x) ::: x can register to vote in the United States
participate_in_election(x) ::: x can participate in the 2024 United States presidential election
us_citizenship(x) ::: x has United States citizenship
taiwanese_citizenship(x) ::: x has Taiwanese citizenship
russian_federation_official(x) ::: x is a Russian Federation official
manager_at_gazprom(x) ::: x is a manager at Gazprom

PREMISES:
forall x (us_citizenship(x) -> register_to_vote(x))
forall x (register_to_vote(x) -> participate_in_election(x))
forall x (us_citizenship(x) v taiwanese_citizenship(x))
not (russian_federation_official(vladimir) & taiwanese_citizenship(vladimir))
register_to_vote(ekaterina)
not (russian_federation_official(ekaterina) & manager_at_gazprom(ekaterina))

CONCLUSION:
participate_in_election(ekaterina) v manager_at_gazprom(ekaterina)

participateInUSPresidentialElection(x) ::: x can participate in the 2024 United States presidential election

managerAtGazprom(x) ::: x is a manager at Gazprom

forall x (registerToVoteInUS(x) -> participateInUSPresidentialElection(x))
forall x (USCitizenship(x) v TaiwaneseCitizenship(x))
NOT (RussianFederationOfficial(vladimir) AND TaiwaneseCitizenship(vladimir))
registerToVoteInUS(ekaterina)
OR (RussianFederationOfficial(ekaterina) AND managerAtGazprom(ekaterina))

CONCLUSION:
participateInUSPresidentialElection(ekaterina) v managerAtGazprom(ekaterina)

All people who fulfill their daily nutritional intakes grew up in health-conscious childhood homes.
All people who disregard their physical well-being are not very conscious about their health and eating habits.
If people visit the gym at least once a day, then they always fulfill their daily nutritional intakes.

conclusion: Taylor eats salads regularly.

disregards_physical_wellbeing(x) ::: x disregards their physical well-being
visits_gym(x) ::: x visits the gym at least once a day
